success_surface: Formula-based text extraction with delimiter normalization
effective_behaviors: Conducted exploratory analysis to verify data structure before implementing solution

Constructed Excel formula with edge case handling for missing trailing delimiters
Used string concatenation within formula to normalize delimiter patterns consistently

memory_items.title: Pre-Solution Data Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always examine workbook structure and sample data values before implementing changes
memory_items.generalization: Use initial exploratory code to verify row counts, column headers, and sample cell contents to inform formula or transformation logic design

memory_items.title: Delimiter Normalization in Formulas
memory_items.lesson: When parsing delimited strings via formula, normalize the input to ensure consistent delimiter presence
memory_items.generalization: Concatenate the delimiter to the source value within the formula itself (e.g., append separator) to handle edge cases where trailing delimiters may be missing

memory_items.title: Formula-Focused Tool Selection
memory_items.lesson: For tasks requiring Excel formula insertion or preservation, prioritize openpyxl over pandas
memory_items.generalization: Use openpyxl when the goal is to write formula strings to cells rather than compute values programmatically in Python

edits.content:
### 7. Formula Construction Best Practices
- **Delimeter Handling**: When extracting substrings between delimiters via formula, concatenate a guaranteed delimiter to the source value to normalize edge cases (e.g., missing trailing separators).
- **Multi-Function Formulas**: Combine text functions (LEFT, MID, RIGHT), position finders (FIND, SEARCH), and cleaner functions (TRIM, LEN) for robust string parsing.
- **Single Cell Targeting**: For cell-level manipulations, apply formulas to precise target cells without altering adjacent data unless specified.
